parsed_review.summary: CP00-204 (RP06 DMS Core "Permission And Audit Binding", risk class A, 10 units RP06.P02.M05.S01-S10) is a descriptor-only/synthetic-only evidence pack and its pack-level production_ready claim is well-supported by implementation, contract, validators, and tests. Verified by direct source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash): (1) service.js freezeCp204Result and createDmsCoreCp204PermissionAuditGateSet build frozen descriptors with synthetic_only/no_real_data true and runtime_execution false on every gate; normalizeDmsCoreServiceRequest uses synthetic tenant/matter/permission/audit defaults and explicitly excludes raw payloads, document bytes, extracted text, permission-decision detail, and audit event bodies. (2) registry.js DMS_CORE_CP204_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION extends the CP203 cascade adding evaluates_authorization_policy_runtime=false, writes_permission_decision=false, appends_audit_trace=false, exposes_policy_rule_detail=false, exposes_audit_hint_detail=false; CP204_REQUIREMENTS enumerate 8 no-leak guards and 7 required public exports. (3) contracts/dms-core-contract.json current_pack=CP00-204 with a permission_audit_workflow_binding_descriptor that matches service.js output; validators.js validateDmsCoreCp204Coverage + validateDmsCoreCp204PermissionAuditWorkflowBinding exhaustively assert descriptor-only, no-leak, and authority-boundary invariants, and the Hermes evidence packet gates production_ready_candidate on both being valid; model.test.js carries 3 CP204 tests. (4) Unit distribution in the manifest matches the baseline exactly ({contract:1,implementation:6,security_audit:2,ui:1}; {"Permission And Audit Binding":10}) with deviation_from_plan=false. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte valid JSON result, stderr 0 bytes), prompt is 2152B (<25.6KB), allowed tools are exactly [Read,Grep,Glob], summary-only review is correctly prohibited (security/audit deliverables present) and deep source inspection was performed. The authority boundary is preserved throughout: every descriptor and packet asserts claude_is_final_approval=false / read_only=true, requires human final approval for runtime opening, disclaims enterprise trust from local validation, and keeps Hermes patterns to deterministic review packet/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false) rather than product runtime. All evidence files (manifest, command-evidence, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are correctly in the pre-review PENDING state with production_ready=false. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 transparency note: as a read-only reviewer I confirmed all 11 listed validation commands exist as npm scripts and that rp06:dms-core:validate encodes the correct CP00-204 assertions, but could not execute them; that evidence is captured during the post-review final_closeout_validation step. This verdict is independent read-only review input only and is not final approval; human final approval remains required before any enterprise-trust/production claim and commit.

findings.title: Local validation command evidence not yet captured at read-only review time
findings.details: The review prompt lists 11 validation commands expected before closeout (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check). At review time these are not recorded as executed: command-evidence.json and construction-inspection.json tests_and_validation.commands are empty arrays. As the read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob only, no Bash) I verified that every one of these commands exists as a real npm script in package.json and that scripts/validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs (rp06:dms-core:validate) asserts the CP00-204 pack id, the {contract:1,implementation:6,security_audit:2,ui:1} distribution, and the descriptor-only / no-leak / authority-boundary invariants, but I did not and cannot execute them. production_ready remains correctly false across manifest, claude-review-result, and construction-inspection.
findings.recommendation: During the documented post-review final_closeout_validation step, execute and capture the full transcript of the 11 validation commands (including a clean npm test with the CP00-204 source present and git diff --check) into command-evidence.json and construction-inspection.json before flipping production_ready to true and committing under human final approval.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected per the hardened_review_sequence: final_closeout_validation runs after the read-only review (review steps 3-7; validation is step 8, commit step 9). Non-blocking and does not affect the descriptor-only/no-real-data/no-leak conclusions; resolved by the post-review validation and commit steps under human final approval.
